message:
fix(runtime): harden NaN propagation guards across statistical subsystems

Adds defensive NaN checks to prevent silent corruption in four
statistical/control-theory components that process floating-point
telemetry data. NaN inputs can arise from division-by-zero in
upstream metric collection (e.g. zero-sample-count means, degenerate
timer ratios), and without guards they propagate through the entire
state, permanently corrupting accumulators and control signals.

alloc_budget.rs (AllocLeakDetector):
  - Guards log_factor against NaN before exponentiation in the
    e-process update. A NaN residual (from degenerate standardization)
    would otherwise produce NaN * e_value, permanently poisoning the
    leak detector's e-value accumulator.

budget.rs (PidState + EProcessState):
  - PidState::update: Returns 0.0 control signal immediately on NaN
    error input, preventing NaN from infecting the integral and
    derivative accumulators which would make the PID controller
    permanently unresponsive.
  - EProcessState: Wraps the e-value multiplication and clamping in
    a NaN guard on log_factor. Previously, a NaN log_factor would
    set e_value to NaN, breaking all subsequent threshold comparisons.

bocpd.rs (BocpdDetector):
  - Config sanitization: All clamp/max calls on config fields
    (min_observation_ms, max_observation_ms, steady_threshold,
    burst_threshold, burst_prior) now check for NaN first and
    substitute sensible defaults, because f64::max(NaN, x) returns
    NaN and f64::clamp with NaN bounds is undefined.
  - Posterior odds: Guards the p_burst calculation against NaN from
    Inf/Inf or 0/0 posterior odds ratios, falling back to 0.5
    (maximum uncertainty) or 1.0 (for infinite odds).

voi_sampling.rs (VoiSampler):
  - All config field sanitizations (prior_alpha, prior_beta, mu_0,
    lambda, value_scale, boundary_weight, sample_cost) check for
    NaN before applying max/clamp, substituting the lower bound or
    a neutral default. This prevents a single NaN config value from
    cascading through the Beta-Bernoulli VOI computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
